description_long: This is a dark, roughly ovoid stone with a matte, uneven surface and visible chipping, suggesting it has been shaped or worked by hand. The stone is predominantly black with small, lighter inclusions scattered across its surface, giving it a speckled appearance. One side features a slightly flattened area with a handwritten white marking, possibly an inventory or catalog number. The edges are irregular and jagged, with some sharper points and a generally asymmetrical profile. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings. Its size and form suggest it could serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban landscape.
description: A dark, roughly ovoid stone with a matte, chipped surface and scattered light inclusions, featuring a white catalog marking on one side.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest side of the stone, highlighting its irregular, chipped surface and the white catalog marking.
description_view_1: This edge-on view reveals the stone's thin profile and jagged, uneven edges.
description_view_2: The opposite broad side is visible here, showing a smoother surface with fewer markings and inclusions.
description_view_3: Another edge-on view, similar to view 1, emphasizing the stone's narrowness and rough contour.
attribute_pattern: Scattered light inclusions and chipped, irregular surface with a hand-written marking; no regular or artificial pattern.
attribute_weathering: Rough and chipped, with visible signs of manual shaping and minor surface wear, but no significant erosion or biological growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or manicured urban surfaces, likely placed intentionally as a visual or physical barrier.
